title: Special characters in comments in Quadlet causes infinite loop in generator
body:
### Issue Description

Special characters string causes infinite loop (100% cpu usage in quadlet to unit files generator):

Seems similar to #21109

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create some test quadlet file: test.container
2. Insert comment to the top of the file: ```# =3;\```
3. Run /usr/libexec/podman/quadlet -v -no-kmsg-log -dryrun
4. The process will get stuck
5. Change the special string (comment) to ```# 3;\```
6. Re-run the quadlet parser which will pass fine

### Describe the results you received

100% CPU usage, quadlet command stuck

Nota bene:
**It looks like this happens only if the quadlet file is the LAST one to be processed - if the Quadlet file with this issue is not the last one to process it get SKIPPED SILENTLY by the quadlet command.**

### Describe the results you expected

Create unit files from quadlet

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

```
[root@localhost quadlets]# cat /etc/redhat-release
AlmaLinux release 9.4 (Seafoam Ocelot)

[root@localhost quadlets]# date
Tue Jun 11 13:10:54 CEST 2024
[root@localhost quadlets]# rpm -q podman
podman-4.9.4-3.el9_4.x86_64
```

### Additional information

The issue happens every time when the scenario is re-run.

comments.body: Thanks for reporting this issue. It was already resolved by #21556 and the fix is part of v5.0.0
